teachingHint: Your child is learning four new sounds: 'g' says g (a short sound at the back of the throat), 'o' says oh (like the start of 'octopus'), 'c' says c (same sound as 'k'), 'k' says k (same sound as 'c'). They can now read words with all the letters learned so far. Help them point to each letter, say its sound, then blend: d-o-g → dog. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!
